text: The priority should only be editable if the task is NOT done/complete

proposedTasks.title: Disable priority editing for completed tasks in Execute details sidebar
proposedTasks.description:
When rendering the priority dropdown in the Execute details sidebar, check the task's status. If the task status is 'closed' (done/complete), the priority field should be read-only and not present the editable dropdown. Acceptance criteria:
- Priority field is editable (shows dropdown on click) for tasks with status 'open' or 'in_progress'
- Priority field is non-editable (displayed as static text, no hover/click affordance) for tasks with status 'closed'
- Visual styling clearly distinguishes the disabled state (e.g., muted text, no pointer cursor)
- If a task transitions to closed while the sidebar is open, the priority field updates to read-only without requiring a page refresh (via WebSocket or state update)

taskTitles: Disable priority editing for completed tasks in Execute details sidebar
